Nitrogen Provided by Organic Fertilization

Source: R/calc_N_from_organic_fertilizer.R
organic_fertilization.Rd

Calculates the amount of nitrogen (N) supplied to the soil through organic fertilization. Optionally applies DPI efficiency by material type, soil distribution_efficiency are both given). If either is NULL, falls back to f.table: value * quantity / 100. Function N_balance() passes computed soil texture and defaults distribution to "medium" so Forg matches the DPI path used by Fert_Office (Apporti al terreno). In the balance formula the term to subtract is the efficient N (N utile) available to the crop in the year of application, not total N (DPI Guida / FertDPI).

Usage

organic_fertilization(
  source = "Cattle slurry",
  frequency = "every year",
  quantity = 100,
  soil_group = NULL,
  distribution_efficiency = NULL,
  f.table = nfert_data_get("f.table")
)

Arguments

source

The source of organic fertilizer (e.g., "Cattle slurry", "Composted manure").

frequency

The frequency of application (e.g., "every year", "every two years").

quantity

The amount of organic fertilizer applied per application (in m3/ha cubic metres, or t/ha). Use 0 for no organic application (returns 0 kg N/ha).

soil_group

Optional. Soil group from calc_soil_group_and_id_rag() (e.g. "Sandy textures", "Franco") to use DPI efficiency by texture. If NULL, efficiency is taken from f.table (no texture/distribution).

distribution_efficiency

Optional. "efficient", "medium", or "low" (DPI: Alta/Media/Bassa). Required together with soil_group for the DPI efficiency path.

f.table

A data frame with columns source, frequency, value. Used when soil_group is NULL. Default is NFert::f.table.

Value

The estimated amount of nitrogen (N) in kg/ha available to the crop (efficient N when soil_group and distribution_efficiency are set).

References

DPI Emilia-Romagna, Guida alla fertilizzazione; efficienza in funzione di tipologia di effluente, tessitura e modalita di distribuzione (Tabella 7 Norme Generali).

Examples

organic_fertilization(source = "Cattle slurry", frequency = "every year", quantity = 50)
#> [1] 0.15
organic_fertilization("Cattle slurry", "every year", 80,
  soil_group = "Sandy textures", distribution_efficiency = "efficient")
#> [1] 241.56
